mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and construction activity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks and reduced corporate output, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >50% of industrial production contraction months during high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 215 reflect entrenched inflationary pressures that compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 215.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months with positive payroll growth over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly real personal income signals weakening household purchasing power relative to aggregate output, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDP-income divergence metric yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during mid-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production drop exceeding 1.0 percent signals severe manufacturing contraction that immediately erodes equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where industrial production falls by more than 1.0 percent.
